channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Hari Raj and Eric Schmitt

item.title: As Israel Loses Support in the U.S., Rahm Emanuel Criticizes Netanyahu
item.description: The former Chicago mayor, a Democrat exploring a 2028 presidential run, is in Tel Aviv calling for an end to unconditional U.S. support of Israel.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rahm Emanuel, the former Chicago mayor and White House chief of staff, is trying to chart a course between the anti-Israel left and the pro-Israel right.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
item.description: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Negotiations between Iran and the United States have been paused until after multiday funeral ceremonies in Tehran for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader who was killed on the first day of the war.

item.title: The Symbols We Saw at Khamenei’s Funeral
item.description: Abdi Latif Dahir, a Middle East correspondent for The Times, explains the messages behind recurring symbols at the dayslong funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Michael Anthony Adams, Mikhail Galustov, Rebecca Suner, Jon Hazell, Joey Sendaydiego and Rafaela Balster
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Lawsuit Alleges Trump Administration Gave Iran Details on Asylum Seekers
item.description: A new lawsuit filed in the District of Columbia states that the administration allowed Iranian officials to “select” which Iranians seeking refuge in the United States would be expelled.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard showing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the slain Iranian leader, at an intersection in Tehran in May.

item.title: A Supreme Leader’s Final Crowds
item.description: What you could see when Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin was being driven to a public space in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times

item.title: Mass Mournings In Qom, Where Khamenei Ruled Supreme Over Iran’s Clerics
item.description: The ceremony for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, held at a site considered sacred by many Shiites, was the latest step in a weeklong funeral organized by Iran’s government.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ahmadreza Taheri/WANA News Agency, via Reuters
item.media:description: A state media handout photo is said to show mourners attending a prayer for Iran’s former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, at the Jamkaran Mosque, in Qom, Iran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Macron Continues With Syria Visit Despite Explosions Near His Hotel in Damascus
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France signaled confidence in Syria’s leaders by staying overnight in the capital and carrying on with his visit despite blasts near his hotel.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Reham Mourshed
item.media:credit: Omar Albam/Associated Press

item.title: Iran’s Strikes on Ships in Strait of Hormuz Threaten Oil Flow
item.description: The strikes by Iran and the attacks and re-imposition of sanctions by the U.S. came weeks after the countries agreed to try to find a lasting end to their hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Elke Scholiers/Getty Images
item.media:description: Vessels anchored near Muscat, Oman, last month.

item.title: What Our Reporter Saw in Tehran: A Human Tide of Mourners
item.description: After days of public mourning ceremonies in the Iranian capital, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin arrived in Tehran’s Azadi Square. This is what I saw covering the miles-long procession on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Emile Ducke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Crowds gathered around the coffin of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran, Iran, on Monday.

item.title: Iranians Mourn a Slain Leader, and Call for Vengeance
item.description: Million of people filled the streets of Iran to bid farewell to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in the war with the United States and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Crowds gathering around Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s casket in Tehran on Monday. The supreme leader’s body will be taken to several cities before being buried in Mashhad, Iran.

item.title: Turkey Hosts NATO Summit While Cracking Down on Critics
item.description: In the weeks leading up to the two-day event, the Turkish authorities blocked websites, jailed scores of people over allegations of terrorism and arrested a prominent comedian.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Yasin Akgul/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Protesters demanding the release of Deniz Goktas, a comedian who was arrested after making jokes about President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey.

item.title: At Khamenei’s funeral, mourners call for revenge.
item.description: Mourners
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Signs calling for revenge against President Trump were held up by people attending the funeral procession on Monday.

item.title: Families of Iranians Killed by Government Dismayed by Leader’s Funeral Pageantry
item.description: Critics of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s brutal rule have been angered by the scale of the commemorations being held to honor his life.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Rozhin Rezai
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Murad Sezer/Reuters
item.media:description: Mourners attending the funeral procession of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Monday surround a monument in Tehran.

item.title: Iran to Bury Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Holy City of Mashhad
item.description: The city of three million people is home to a gold-domed shrine that Shiite Muslims revere as Iran’s most sacred site. The final ceremony will take place there on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mashhad is Iran’s second most populous city and where Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was born.

item.title: Former Syrian Officials Found Guilty in Torture of Pro-Democracy Protesters
item.description: The case against the two men, who have been living in Austria since 2015, was one of the first that the country has brought under the principle of universal jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall and Hussam Hammoud
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Marylise Vigneau for The New York Times
item.media:description: Khaled al-Halabi, a former intelligence commander under the dictator Bashar al-Assad, after a hearing in Vienna last month. Mr. al-Halabi was convicted of torture among other charges.

item.title: The Iranian authorities go all out to stage an elaborate weeklong spectacle.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at the Grand Mosalla mosque in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Ships Are Moving In and Out of the Persian Gulf, Easing Oil Prices
item.description: A recovery in oil flows from the Persian Gulf and a pledge by OPEC Plus to pump more crude have put downward pressure on energy prices.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross, Jason Karaian and Rich Barbieri
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Khamenei’s funeral draws mourners from across Iran’s regional network.
item.description: From Lebanon to cities across Pakistan, gatherings and marches are expected to show his reach.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at the Grand Mosalla mosque in Tehran on Saturday for the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former supreme leader.

item.title: As Mourners for Khamenei Descend on Tehran, Many Residents Take Flight
item.description: Some residents have escaped the city and posted photos of their getaways on social media — a reminder that not all Iranians are mourning the death of Ayatollah Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: As Trump Cools on NATO, Turkey Hosts Summit Meeting
item.description: As President Trump cools on the alliance, its members see Turkey’s large military and vibrant defense sector as assets.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.media:credit: Efekan Akyuz/Reuters
item.media:description: Workers in the Turkish capital, Ankara. Turkey will use the NATO summit to showcase the country’s status as a rising power.

item.title: Erdogan-Trump Friendship Can Help NATO, Turkey’s Foreign Minister Says
item.description: In an interview, Turkey’s top diplomat, Hakan Fidan, said the relationship between President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and President Trump could ease NATO tensions.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump with President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Turkey in the White House, last year. While directly criticizing some NATO leaders, Mr. Trump has maintained cordial ties with Mr. Erdogan.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Abdi Latif Dahir

item.title: Israel’s Government Threatens to Ignore a Top Court Ruling
item.description: A resolution by the country’s cabinet escalated a long-running conflict between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and the country’s judiciary. A senior government official later walked that back.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Johnatan Reiss
item.media:credit: Ilia Yefimovich/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at a public event on Sunday with his wife, Sara.

item.title: At Site Where Iran’s Slain Leader Lies, Some See a Symbol of His Failures
item.description: The complex where mourners are paying their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this weekend is unfinished after nearly 40 years. Critics say it is emblematic of the unfulfilled promises of his rule.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Grand Mosalla complex in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Exhausted by Iran War, Tehran Transforms for Khamenei’s Funeral
item.description: Throughout the capital, the government is using Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral to project strength at a time of great uncertainty for Iran.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Emile Ducke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People in central Tehran on Saturday, below an image of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as a younger man. Portraits of the slain leader can be seen across the capital.

item.title: OPEC Plus Pledges to Pump More Even as Oil Prices Fall
item.description: The cartel’s decision comes as U.S.-Iran talks to end the war have eased pressure on oil supplies, even as broader market and political volatility persists.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ahmed Jadallah/Reuters
item.media:description: An oil refinery in Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia, in 2018. The country has been actively ramping up oil shipments since the United States and Iran signed a framework agreement in June.

item.title: Mourners Chant ‘Revenge’ at Funeral Prayers for Iran’s Slain Supreme Leader
item.description: On the second day of public mourning for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in February, there was still no sign of his son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran, on Sunday.

item.title: What We Saw at Ayatollah Khamenei’s Funeral
item.description: Our Middle East correspondent Abdi Latif Dahir attended the first day of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the former supreme leader of Iran who was killed by U.S. and Israeli air strikes in February.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Michael Anthony Adams, Mikhail Galustov, Coleman Lowndes and Rebecca Suner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Huge Crowds Mass in Tehran for Ayatollah’s State Funeral
item.description: As days of public mourning ceremonies began, Iranians viewed the casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners gathered in Tehran to pay their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei for his funeral on Saturday.

item.title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: The funeral comes amid fraught, sluggish U.S.-Iran peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz has been a key point of U.S.-Iran negotiations.

item.title: When Iran Last Buried a Supreme Leader, There Was Chaos
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.media:credit: Kaveh Kazemi/Getty Images
item.media:description: Crowds of mourners surrounding the body of Ayatollah Khomeini in a container in Tehran, on June 6, 1989.

item.title: Will Iran’s New Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, Attend His Father’s Funeral?
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since March, after his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed in an Israeli strike.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A funeral ceremony on Friday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Mosalla complex in Tehran. His son and successor, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, is in hiding.

item.title: What to Know About Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as His Funeral Services Begin in Iran
item.description: Iran’s longstanding supreme leader concentrated power, presided over crackdowns and built an armed proxy network that projected influence across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard of the slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a day before his state funeral in Tehran, on Thursday.

item.title: Momentary Unity at a Funeral Masks Deep Divisions Among Iran’s Leaders
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei’s absence has raised questions inside Iranian political circles about who is really running the country and allowed open divisions to fester.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranian officials standing next to the coffin of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

item.title: Iran Projects Unity to the World While Pursuing a Crackdown at Home
item.description: The country’s security service has continued to target civil society and dissidents, including thousands of arrests since the launch of the U.S.-Israeli war in February.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Aaron Boxerman, Yeganeh Torbati and James C. McKinley Jr.

item.title: Funeral Procession for Khamenei Will Visit 5 Cities in Iran and Iraq
item.description: After lying in state in Tehran, Mr. Khamenei’s body will be taken to some of the holiest sites in Shiite Islam during a four-day procession that is expected to draw millions.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Preparations being made for the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on Thursday.

item.title: It’s Day 1 of the Supreme Leader’s Funeral, and Allies Gather in Iran
item.description: Foreign dignitaries paid their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Friday as days of ceremonies got underway.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dignitaries walking past the coffins of Iran’s slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and his family members after paying their respects at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Why One Global Shipping Choke Point Isn’t a Blueprint for Another
item.description: The Strait of Malacca may be a model for how Oman and Iran could collect fees in the Strait of Hormuz, but the differences between the waterways are vast.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Cheng Yiheng/Xinhua, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Some 100,000 vessels pass through the Strait of Malacca a year.

item.title: U.S. Officials Believed Israel Was Plotting to Kill Iranian Negotiators
item.description: Any Israeli attempt to kill Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister, or Mohammad Ghalibaf, the Parliament speaker, would have derailed peace talks, American officials feared.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Urs Flueeler
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, left, with Abbas Araghchi, center, traveled to Switzerland in June for a second in-person meeting with Vice President JD Vance and the American delegation.

item.title: Bomb Explodes at Damascus Cafe, Killing at Least 6
item.description: The blast, near the Syrian capital’s main courthouse, underscored the country’s persistent instability as its leaders try to chart a path out of years of dictatorship and war.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Reham Mourshed
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Louai Beshara/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Syrian security forces at the site of an explosion at a cafe in Damascus on Thursday.

item.title: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Killed at Iran War’s Outset
item.description: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers in Tehran assembled scaffolding on Thursday as the country prepared for the funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, whose image adorned the side of a nearby building.

item.title: U.S. Hopes for Investment in Iran Would Break With Decades of History
item.description: Senior U.S. officials have said Iran would be richly rewarded for changing its stance on the United States. Iran’s leaders have rejected such a bargain in the past.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Grand Bazaar in Iran’s capital, Tehran, in May. The U.S. has offered Iran sanctions relief if it agrees to limits on its nuclear program.

item.title: U.S. Restarts Shipments of Dollars to Iraq, Pausing Pressure
item.description: The Trump administration had halted the deliveries as part of its efforts to pressure the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Falih Hassan
item.media:credit: Hadi Mizban/Associated Press
item.media:description: Iraq’s prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, has taken steps to try to rein in Iran-backed militias in his country.
